other_observers: Cathy Sheeter, who first found bird;
description: At Ft Collins Museum of Discovery on 6 January 2013. The undertail covert was limited to one short streak, the rump unstreaked. The side streaking was paler than that of most (all?) male CORE present, but the extent of streaking was only modestly limited in c/w CORE males. The red on crown was more limited in extent than that of male CORE and the black on face more restricted as well, and not as black. The bill was smaller in all dimensions than that of most CORE, and thus not proportionately shorter, so that the "pushed-in" look is not apparent. This bird scored 15.5 on Sibley's scale, putting it well into the Hoary range.Behaviors: At feeders with other redpollsCall: unknownPlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow
